title: Kashmiri Rogan Josh

subs.name: bone-in chicken pieces — reduce simmering to 35 minutes
subs.notes: rogan josh is traditionally lamb or mutton but chicken absorbs the spices beautifully

subs.name: 100 ml thick coconut cream added after searing
subs.notes: stir in the same way to prevent splitting; the dish will taste less tangy

ingredients.name: Kashmiri red chilli powder
visual_checks: use Kashmiri mirch for deep colour without excessive heat
ingredients.notes: use Kashmiri mirch for deep colour without excessive heat

ingredients.name: dried whole Kashmiri red chillies, soaked 10 min
dried ginger powder (sonth)
visual_checks: dry ginger powder; do not substitute with fresh ginger
ingredients.notes: dry ginger powder; do not substitute with fresh ginger

subs.name: ghee (same quantity)
subs.notes: ghee gives a richer, less pungent base

steps.text: Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over high flame until it turns pale and smokes lightly. Add the bay leaves, cardamom, cloves and cinnamon — let them sizzle for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the mutton pieces and sear on high heat, turning every 2 minutes, until well browned all over, about 10 minutes. The browning builds the foundational flavour of the dish.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add the Kashmiri chilli powder, dried ginger powder and fennel seeds. Stir for 1 minute to coat the meat without letting the spices burn.

Squeeze the soaked dried chillies and add them to the pot. Add the whisked curd one tablespoon at a time, stirring constantly after each addition so the curd does not split. Once all the curd is incorporated, stir vigorously for 2 minutes on medium flame.

Pour in 300 ml water and season with salt. Bring to a boil, then cover tightly and reduce to a low simmer. Cook for 60 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes and adding a small splash of water only if the pot looks dry.

Uncover and check the mutton — it should be fork-tender and the oil should be floating in pools of deep red on the surface. If the gravy is too thin, simmer uncovered for 10 more minutes to concentrate it.
Taste for salt and rest 10 minutes before serving. Rogan josh is served with steamed basmati rice or Kashmiri naan — the rich red gravy stains the rice a pale coral when mixed.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mutton → bone-in chicken pieces — reduce simmering to 35 minutes (rogan josh is traditionally lamb or mutton but chicken absorbs the spices beautifully); mustard-oil → ghee (same quantity) (ghee gives a richer, less pungent base); curd → 100 ml thick coconut cream added after searing (stir in the same way to prevent splitting; the dish will taste less tangy)
